messages.author: Jude
messages.text: I have an interesting idea. What if we could do very slow links of voice over Lora? Specifically Meshcore. Based on this for MC on the new narrow settings I have around 2.5 kps to work with. Not much, But based on this blog https://www.rowetel.com/wordpress/?page_id=452 with codec 2 I should be able to get a voice compression all the way down to 700 bits per second thats .7kps. So theoretically I could do around 3 links of voice over meshcore. Not much. But if we limit it so only one person can transmit at the same time like how when you log into a Ham repeater over your phone app and your call sign only one person can transmit at the same time. The only issue I could see with this is if one node repeats the message and the message comes back to you does it take double the bandwidth? Like if I can only do 3 links before we run out of bandwidth does that mean I can only do 3 hops? I want to implement this into a way that I have a custom chanel that people can send voice messages over and the message is then stored on the phone untill someone plays it back
messages.content_html: I have an interesting idea. What if we could do very slow links of voice over Lora? Specifically Meshcore. Based on this for MC on the new narrow settings I have around 2.5 kps to work with. Not much, But based on this blog https://www.rowetel.com/wordpress/?page_id=452 with codec 2 I should be able to get a voice compression all the way down to 700 bits per second thats .7kps. So theoretically I could do around 3 links of voice over meshcore. Not much. But if we limit it so only one person can transmit at the same time like how when you log into a Ham repeater over your phone app and your call sign only one person can transmit at the same time. The only issue I could see with this is if one node repeats the message and the message comes back to you does it take double the bandwidth? Like if I can only do 3 links before we run out of bandwidth does that mean I can only do 3 hops? I want to implement this into a way that I have a custom chanel that people can send voice messages over and the message is then stored on the phone untill someone plays it back

messages.author: alaspooryorick
messages.text: one person speaking at a time is still that amount of bandwidth over the entire time transmitting, as opposed to relatively short time text packets are live for

messages.author: Emcee
messages.text: Rather than sending voice waveforms, how about a voice-to-text-to-voice cycle? It would be nice to have the ability to send/receive msg without having to look at a screen. Both components independently would be useful actually, but linked together would achieve some "voice" functionality without high bandwidth requirements or departure from the short msg ethos.
messages.content_html: Rather than sending voice waveforms, how about a voice-to-text-to-voice cycle? It would be nice to have the ability to send/receive msg without having to look at a screen. Both components independently would be useful actually, but linked together would achieve some "voice" functionality without high bandwidth requirements or departure from the short msg ethos.
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: alaspooryorick
messages.text: right now much of meshcore feels targeted at keeping the app/companion setup model, so I don't know that many/any users would get a benefit if their node started talking to them, and then I don't think many people want to be jump scared when a random person walks into range and starts spamming "anyone out there?" messages at 2 am
messages.content_html: right now much of meshcore feels targeted at keeping the app/companion setup model, so I don't know that many/any users would get a benefit if their node started talking to them, and then I don't think many people want to be jump scared when a random person walks into range and starts spamming "anyone out there?" messages at 2 am
messages.reactions_html: 3
